2025-01-06.text: There's a lot of bullshit rumors about TSMC being spread regarding 2nm, Apple, Nvidia, and Qualcomm TSMC 2nm N2 has no delays It was always H2 2025 for high volume manufacturing, as stated by TSMC publicly in 2023 TSMC switched to >2 year schedule between major nodes in 2021 1/4

Nvidia and Qualcomm have no faith in Samsung Samsung is~3 years behind TSMC in PPA at this point, and even ~1 year behind Intel Nvidia and Qualcomm always test new nodes at Samsung, and now also testing Intel Neither plans to move their highest volume products away from TSMC 4/4

Apple will release their first products with N2 in 2026, on schedule, as planned They are increasing use of N-1 nodes with SoIC in their high end APs to keep costs down despite large wafer price increases Apple's focused on wide IO copackaged memory to keep perf gains going  3/4

Indium Phosphide (InP) is a III-V compound semiconductor, made from indium (group III) and phosphorus (group V). It is the substrate material that every datacenter laser chip is fabricated on. Every optical transceiver connecting GPUs in AI clusters uses InP laser dies inside.

InP is used because it is the only III-V material whose crystal lattice allows you to grow alloys that emit light at 1310nm and 1550nm, the two wavelengths where optical fiber has minimum signal loss and minimum dispersion. The material choice is dictated by physics, not

2025-01-06.text: There's a lot of bullshit rumors about TSMC being spread regarding 2nm, Apple, Nvidia, and Qualcomm TSMC 2nm N2 has no delays It was always H2 2025 for high volume manufacturing, as stated by TSMC publicly in 2023 TSMC switched to >2 year schedule between major nodes in 2021 1/4

Nvidia and Qualcomm have no faith in Samsung Samsung is~3 years behind TSMC in PPA at this point, and even ~1 year behind Intel Nvidia and Qualcomm always test new nodes at Samsung, and now also testing Intel Neither plans to move their highest volume products away from TSMC 4/4

Apple will release their first products with N2 in 2026, on schedule, as planned They are increasing use of N-1 nodes with SoIC in their high end APs to keep costs down despite large wafer price increases Apple's focused on wide IO copackaged memory to keep perf gains going  3/4
